summary: PR #2510 matched `mnEvent_8024CE74` by restoring a local `static inline` count helper in `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`. The PR body says the helper changes MWCC local initialization codegen from two independent `li` instructions to the expected dependent register copy, and cites analogous `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` / `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` patterns in `mn/`. Review feedback rejected unrelated/earlier data and assert-override work around `mnnamenew` static literals, recommending landing only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` code match and not chasing data sections before code is matched.

key_files.role: Final changed file: added `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` and changed `mnEvent_8024CE74` to call it after the debug-level early return.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `mnEvent_CountUnlocked` added; `PAD_STACK(8)` and duplicate `count = 0` removed; `mnEvent_8024CE74` calls helper

key_files.role: Reviewed but not present in final changed-file metadata: reviewer objected to temporary `HSD_ASSERT` override / `jobj.h` include changes as premature sdata work.
Reviewed but not present in final changed-file metadata: reviewer objected to extern declarations for static literals.

what_changed: Restored a `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` helper containing the `for (i = 0; i < 0x33; i++)` loop and `gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i) != 0` count increment.
Changed `mnEvent_8024CE74` to keep the `g_debugLevel > 2` early return, then assign `count = mnEvent_CountUnlocked();` before the threshold checks.
Removed `PAD_STACK(8)` from the shown hunk and changed local declarations from `int` to `s32` in the restored helper/caller shape.

PR body reports verification: `python configure.py && ninja` green, `mnEvent_8024CE74` improved from 99.4%/99.42% to 100%, and no other functions affected; bot report records one new match in `main/melee/mn/mnevent`.
smart_moves: Used a small source-level helper to express an apparent authored pattern rather than keeping padding or a generated-looking inline loop in the caller.
Preserved the debug-level early return in `mnEvent_8024CE74` and isolated the count loop in a descriptive local static inline.
Grounded the helper shape in claimed local analogs: `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` elsewhere in `mn/`.
Final changed-file metadata shows only `mnevent.c`, consistent with reviewer guidance to separate the code match from unrelated data/sdata cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC, restoring a likely original `static inline` helper can affect register dependency and initialization lowering after inlining; in this PR, the body says grouping `i` and `count` inside `mnEvent_CountUnlocked` produced the expected `addi r30, r31, 0` instead of two independent `li` instructions.

When matching repeated subsystem patterns, look for sibling helper names and shapes before compensating with stack padding or local churn; this PR cites `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` / `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` as same-pattern evidence for `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`.

Do not mix a focused text-section function match with premature data-section or static-literal declaration work; reviewer feedback recommended landing only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` match and leaving TU data-section matching for later human cleanup.
naming_conventions: Count helper restored as `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`, matching the file/subsystem prefix plus action pattern cited alongside `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`.
Local variables in the helper use straightforward roles `i` and `count`, matching the PR body's explanation of the count loop.

No evidence in the final diff supports global data renames or new external names for `mnNameNew_804D4F84` / `mnNameNew_804D4F8C`; reviewer explicitly called them static literals that do not need declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a `static inline` wrapper to influence MWCC inlined local initialization/register-copy shape when local sibling code suggests the helper was authored that way.

The PR body identifies the relevant assembly symptom: inline loop in caller lowered `i = 0` and `count = 0` as two `li` instructions, while expected code used `addi r30, r31, 0` for the second initialization.
Removed/avoided `PAD_STACK(8)` in the final shown `mnevent.c` hunk once the helper restored the match shape.
Verification evidence from PR body and bot report claims one new 100% match for `mnEvent_8024CE74` and no other affected functions.
review_feedback: ribbanya objected to an attempted `mnnamenew.c` assert override around `HSD_ASSERT` / `jobj.h`: "This is slop. At best, it prematurely fixes sdata."
ribbanya objected to `mnnamenew.h` extern declarations for `mnNameNew_804D4F84` and `mnNameNew_804D4F8C`: "These are static literals and don't need to be declared anywhere."
ribbanya recommended merging only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` match and advising Claude to stop worrying about data, noting that a human can match TU data sections once code is matched.
A later review by ribbanya is recorded as APPROVED with an empty body; the final changed-file metadata lists only `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`.
follow_up_queries: Search `src/melee/mn` for `*_CountUnlocked` and `*_CountUnlockedChars` helpers when matching similar menu count loops.
Search for remaining `PAD_STACK` in `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c` or nearby `mn/` functions where a small static inline helper may match local authored style.
Search PR/review history for "prematurely fixes sdata", "static literals", and `HSD_ASSERT` overrides to find other rejected data-section matching attempts.
Search for `mnNameNew_804D4F84` and `mnNameNew_804D4F8C` only as a review caution; do not assume those extern declarations were accepted.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Static inline wrappers can be source-faithful MWCC matching tools for menu count loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2510, restoring `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` around a simple count loop was reported to recover the expected MWCC register-copy initialization (`addi r30, r31, 0`) and match `mnEvent_8024CE74` at 100%. Use local sibling helper patterns as evidence before keeping stack padding or generated-looking local initialization shapes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Why section and helper snippet
raw_diff: helper restored and caller changed
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows one new match for `mnEvent_8024CE74`

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep focused code-match PRs separate from speculative data-section cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2510 review feedback rejected unrelated `mnnamenew` sdata/static-literal work and recommended merging just the `mnEvent_8024CE74` text match. This supports treating data-section changes, assert override hacks, and static-literal extern declarations as separate evidence-backed work rather than routine code-match cleanup.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer local static-inline helper restoration over padding when sibling patterns and MWCC codegen support it
source_update_candidates.text: When a small repeated loop appears inline in a caller but nearby matched code uses same-prefix static inline helpers, try restoring the helper. PR #2510 reports that this can change MWCC local initialization from independent immediates to an expected register-copy dependency after inlining. Require normal verification before accepting the shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report one new match
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a reusable matching tactic, but should be curated into standards only with wording that keeps it evidence- and verification-bound.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not introduce assert macro overrides or static-literal externs as incidental data-section fixes in focused code-match PRs
source_update_candidates.text: Review in PR #2510 called an attempted `HSD_ASSERT` override around `jobj.h` premature sdata work and said static literals such as `mnNameNew_804D4F84` / `mnNameNew_804D4F8C` did not need extern declarations. Focused text matches should avoid unrelated data/sdata churn unless explicitly scoped and evidenced.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: review body recommending humans handle TU data sections once code is matched
source_update_candidates.reason: The point overlaps existing standards on literals/data ownership and text-before-data matching; curator may use this PR as additional historical evidence or example wording.

rejection_notes: Do not record `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c` or `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.h` as final changed files for this PR; final changed-file metadata lists only `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`.
Do not promote `mnNameNew_804D4F84` or `mnNameNew_804D4F8C` extern declarations as accepted names or path facts; reviewer said they were static literals and unnecessary.
Do not infer that all count loops should be static inline helpers; this PR supports the tactic only when MWCC codegen and local sibling patterns provide evidence.
Do not independently claim build success beyond the PR body's and bot report's stated verification; intake did not run tools.
